raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I paid {$330.00} using my Citi Mastercard to XXXX XXXX for a phone screen replacement. The service was supposed to fully replace a cracked screen. However, the work was defective : The new screen was glued on crooked. 

There is dried glue residue visible on the phone. 

After the repair, a new problem appeared : something is loose inside the device and rattles when moved, indicating parts were not properly reassembled during their service. 

I disputed the charge on XX/XX/year> under the XXXX Credit Billing Act, citing service not as agreed. Citi issued a provisional credit, investigated, and later reversed the credit on XX/XX/year>, ruling in favor of the merchant. Citis denial letter states : The services were provided as intended. This is factually incorrect based on the evidence. The device came back in worse condition than before the repair due to their improper work. 

I requested supporting documentation and received XXXX denial letter ( attached ). Their decision disregards clear evidence of defective workmanship, including photos, video, and the original invoice stating the work was a full screen replacement. 

Requested Resolution : I want Citi to permanently credit my account for the disputed amount and remove any related interest or fees, as required under 15 U.S.C. 1666 and Regulation Z for services not delivered as agreed.
